column2: Combined Phase 4+5 in one PR: AuditGateDO is now a generalized persona-shaped agent runtime
column3: Combined Phase 4+5 in one PR. AuditGateDO is now a generalized persona-shaped agent runtime, not a canon-code-sync-specific service. The runtime accepts persona_uri per-invocation (allow-listed against ALLOWED_PERSONA_URIS in worker/src/runtime/audit-gate.ts); knowledge_base_url is derived from the OIDC token's repository claim; system_prompt_uri resolution uses the same URI scheme. Adding a new audit type now means writing a new persona canon doc, adding the URI to the allow-list, and writing a thin caller workflow — no substrate changes.

column2: Authentication is GitHub Actions OIDC only; no shared-secret fallback
column3: Authentication is GitHub Actions OIDC only. No shared-secret fallback. The Worker fetches https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com/.well-known/jwks (cached 1h in isolate global; per-kid re-fetch on miss for rotation tolerance), verifies RS256 signature via WebCrypto, validates iss/aud/exp/iat. The repository claim is allow-listed (klappy/agent-messaging-service, klappy/klappy.dev) at the route level so arbitrary GitHub workflows cannot burn the gate's Anthropic budget. PR owner/repo/sha come from JWT claims, not the request body — a workflow cannot audit a repo it doesn't represent. JWT verification lives in worker/src/runtime/oidc.ts as a reusable substrate module separate from audit-gate.ts.

Workflow split into reusable + caller. .github/workflows/audit-gate-runtime-probe.yml is workflow_call-shaped with inputs persona_uri, comment_header, audit_gate_url, oidc_audience. .github/workflows/canon-code-sync-audit-runtime-probe.yml is the first caller (persona_uri: ams://canon/personas/ams-canon-code-auditor). Future audit types (output validation, oddkit gauntlets) get new caller workflows without touching the reusable.

column2: Hardcoded Phase 4 would have required Phase 5 refactor; operator caught the gap, combining avoids placeholder-survival
column3: Phase 4 as originally drafted was hardcoded to canon-code-sync — would have required a Phase 5 to extract reusability. Operator caught the gap with "We don't use human devs anymore" (drop shared-secret) and B (combine phases). Combining them avoids the placeholder-survival pattern where hardcoded code lands and is later refactored. The reusable shape is the same number of lines as the hardcoded one, just better abstracted at the boundary (route signature + workflow inputs).

column2: Reusable substrate is bounded by code allow-lists, not data; expansion requires PRs the audit gate audits
column3: Reusable runtime substrate is bounded by two allow-lists in code, not data. ALLOWED_PERSONA_URIS in worker/src/runtime/audit-gate.ts and REPOSITORY_ALLOW_LIST in worker/src/index.ts. Both adds require a PR — which means the audit gate audits them. This is intentional: the runtime is reusable by design but expansion requires reviewable, canon-grounded code changes. Free-form persona URIs from the network would make this an open execution surface.

column2: GITHUB_TOKEN threaded ephemerally for private-repo diff fetch
column3: Private-repo diff fetching threads the caller workflow's per-run GITHUB_TOKEN in the request body. The Worker uses it for the diff fetch only and does not persist it (no DO storage write, no logging). The token is scoped to the workflow run that issued it and dies with that run. Public-repo diffs work unauthenticated for backward compatibility with anonymous probes.

column2: Probe workflow non-gating; parity evidence accumulates organically; cutover is one-line workflow swap
column3: The probe workflow posts a clearly-labeled "side-by-side probe — not gating" comment on every PR. The legacy canon-code-sync-audit.yml continues to gate merge. Disagreement between the two paths is the parity signal: when they agree across 2-3 real PRs, the operator decides whether to cut over. The cutover, if it happens, is a one-line workflow swap (the legacy workflow stops dispatching the Managed Agents path; the probe workflow stops being non-gating). No endpoint rename needed — /audit-gate-test stays its current name, and the canon doc acknowledges the test- prefix becomes a misnomer at cutover.

column2: Phase 4+5 PR forthcoming with 9 files; audit gate will self-audit
column3: Phase 4+5 PR forthcoming. 9 files: canon-code-sync-via-spawned-agent-session.md (canon §Current Implementation update, surfaced for operator review and greenlit), worker/src/runtime/audit-gate.ts (persona parameterization + GITHUB_TOKEN + head+tail diff cap + capDiff helper), worker/src/runtime/oidc.ts (new reusable JWT verification module with JWKS cache), worker/src/index.ts (OIDC route, persona+repository allow-lists, JWT-claim-trusted invocation construction), worker/src/types.ts (AMS_AUDIT_GATE_TEST_SECRET dropped), worker/wrangler.toml (secret doc replaced with OIDC notes), .github/workflows/audit-gate-runtime-probe.yml (new reusable), .github/workflows/canon-code-sync-audit-runtime-probe.yml (new caller), journal/2026-05-13-phase-4-5-oidc-reusable-substrate.tsv (this DOLCHEO trail). Local typecheck passes; wrangler v4 dry-run produces 2.3MB bundle. Audit gate will auto-fire on the PR. Expected PASS: canon and code agree (canon doc was authored to describe this exact substrate state).
